heading: Qualified asset account; limitation on additions to account

children.text: disability benefits,
medical benefits,
SUB or severance pay benefits, or
life insurance benefits.

children.heading: Limitation on additions to account
children.label: b Limitation on additions to account
children.text: No addition to any qualified asset account may be taken into account under section 419(c)(1)(B) to the extent such addition results in the amount in such account exceeding the account limit.

children.label: A
children.text: claims incurred but unpaid (as of the close of such taxable year) for benefits referred to in subsection (a), and

children.label: B
children.text: administrative costs with respect to such claims.

children.heading: Additional reserve for post-retirement medical and life insurance benefits
children.label: 2 Additional reserve for post-retirement medical and life insurance benefits
A
children.text: post-retirement medical benefits to be provided to covered employees (determined on the basis of current medical costs), or

children.label: B
children.text: post-retirement life insurance benefits to be provided to covered employees.

children.heading: Amount taken into account for SUB or severance pay benefits
children.label: 3 Amount taken into account for SUB or severance pay benefits

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The account limit for any taxable year with respect to SUB or severance pay benefits is 75 percent of the average annual qualified direct costs for SUB or severance pay benefits for any 2 of the immediately preceding 7 taxable years (as selected by the fund).

children.heading: Special rule for certain new plans
children.label: B Special rule for certain new plans
children.text: In the case of any new plan for which SUB or severance pay benefits are not available to any key employee, the Secretary shall, by regulations, provide for an interim amount to be taken into account under paragraph (1).

children.heading: Limitation on amounts to be taken into account
children.label: 4 Limitation on amounts to be taken into account
i
children.text: 75 percent of such individual’s average compensation for his high 3 years (within the meaning of section 415(b)(3)), or

children.label: ii
children.text: the limitation in effect under section 415(b)(1)(A).

children.heading: Limitation on SUB or severance pay benefits
children.label: B Limitation on SUB or severance pay benefits
children.text: For purposes of paragraph (3), any SUB or severance pay benefit payable to any individual shall not be taken into account to the extent such benefit is payable at an annual rate in excess of 150 percent of the limitation in effect under section 415(c)(1)(A).

children.heading: Special limitation where no actuarial certification
children.label: 5 Special limitation where no actuarial certification

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Unless there is an actuarial certification of the account limit determined under this subsection for any taxable year, the account limit for such taxable year shall not exceed the sum of the safe harbor limits for such taxable year.

children.heading: Short-term disability benefits
children.label: i Short-term disability benefits
children.text: In the case of short-term disability benefits, the safe harbor limit for any taxable year is 17.5 percent of the qualified direct costs (other than insurance premiums) for the immediately preceding taxable year with respect to such benefits.

children.heading: Medical benefits
children.label: ii Medical benefits
children.text: In the case of medical benefits, the safe harbor limit for any taxable year is 35 percent of the qualified direct costs (other than insurance premiums) for the immediately preceding taxable year with respect to medical benefits.

children.heading: SUB or severance pay benefits
children.label: iii SUB or severance pay benefits
children.text: In the case of SUB or severance pay benefits, the safe harbor limit for any taxable year is the amount determined under paragraph (3).

children.heading: Long-term disability or life insurance benefits
children.label: iv Long-term disability or life insurance benefits
children.text: In the case of any long-term disability benefit or life insurance benefit, the safe harbor limit for any taxable year shall be the amount prescribed by regulations.

children.heading: Additional reserve for medical benefits of bona fide association plans
children.label: 6 Additional reserve for medical benefits of bona fide association plans
i
children.text: the qualified direct costs, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the change in claims incurred but unpaid,

children.heading: Applicable account limit
children.label: B Applicable account limit
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “applicable account limit” means an account limit for a qualified asset account with respect to medical benefits provided through a plan maintained by a bona fide association (as defined in section 2791(d)(3) of the Public Health Service Act ()).42 U.S.C. 300gg–91(d)(3)

children.heading: Requirement of separate accounts for post-retirement medical or life insurance benefits provided to key employees
children.label: d Requirement of separate accounts for post-retirement medical or life insurance benefits provided to key employees
A
children.text: a separate account shall be established for any medical benefits or life insurance benefits provided with respect to such employee after retirement, and

children.label: B
children.text: medical benefits and life insurance benefits provided with respect to such employee after retirement may only be paid from such separate account.

children.heading: Coordination with section 415
children.label: 2 Coordination with section 415
children.text: For purposes of section 415, any amount attributable to medical benefits allocated to an account established under paragraph (1) shall be treated as an annual addition to a defined contribution plan for purposes of section 415(c). Subparagraph (B) of section 415(c)(1) shall not apply to any amount treated as an annual addition under the preceding sentence.

children.heading: Key employee
children.label: 3 Key employee
children.text: For purposes of this section, the term “key employee” means any employee who, at any time during the plan year or any preceding plan year, is or was a key employee as defined in section 416(i).

children.heading: Special limitations on reserves for medical benefits or life insurance benefits provided to retired employees
children.label: e Special limitations on reserves for medical benefits or life insurance benefits provided to retired employees

children.heading: Reserve must be nondiscriminatory
children.label: 1 Reserve must be nondiscriminatory
children.text: No reserve may be taken into account under subsection (c)(2) for post-retirement medical benefits or life insurance benefits to be provided to covered employees unless the plan meets the requirements of section 505(b) with respect to such benefits (whether or not such requirements apply to such plan). The preceding sentence shall not apply to any plan maintained pursuant to an agreement between employee representatives and 1 or more employers if the Secretary finds that such agreement is a collective bargaining agreement and that post-retirement medical benefits or life insurance benefits were the subject of good faith bargaining between such employee representatives and such employer or employers.

children.heading: Limitation on amount of life insurance benefits
children.label: 2 Limitation on amount of life insurance benefits
children.text: Life insurance benefits shall not be taken into account under subsection (c)(2) to the extent the aggregate amount of such benefits to be provided with respect to the employee exceeds $50,000.

children.heading: Definitions and other special rules
children.label: f Definitions and other special rules
A
children.text: any supplemental unemployment compensation benefit (as defined in section 501(c)(17)(D)), and

children.label: B
children.text: any severance pay benefit.

children.heading: Medical benefit
children.label: 2 Medical benefit
children.text: The term “medical benefit” means a benefit which consists of the providing (directly or through insurance) of medical care (as defined in section 213(d)).

children.heading: Life insurance benefit
children.label: 3 Life insurance benefit
children.text: The term “life insurance benefit” includes any other death benefit.

children.heading: Valuation
children.label: 4 Valuation
children.text: For purposes of this section, the amount of the qualified asset account shall be the value of the assets in such account (as determined under regulations).

children.heading: Special rule for collective bargained and employee pay-all plans
children.label: 5 Special rule for collective bargained and employee pay-all plans
A
children.text: under a collective bargaining agreement, or

children.label: i
children.text: such plan has at least 50 employees (determined without regard to subsection (h)(1)), and

children.label: ii
children.text: no employee is entitled to a refund with respect to amounts in the fund, other than a refund based on the experience of the entire fund.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: This subpart shall not apply in the case of any welfare benefit fund which is part of a 10 or more employer plan. The preceding sentence shall not apply to any plan which maintains experience-rating arrangements with respect to individual employers.

children.label: i
children.text: to which more than 1 employer contributes, and

children.label: ii
children.text: to which no employer normally contributes more than 10 percent of the total contributions contributed under the plan by all employers.

children.heading: Increase in account limit
children.label: A Increase in account limit
children.text: The account limit for any of the first 4 taxable years to which this section applies shall be increased by the applicable percentage of any existing excess reserves.

children.heading: Applicable percentage
children.label: B Applicable percentage
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A)—In the case of:The applicable percentage is:The first taxable year to which this section applies80The second taxable year to which this section applies60The third taxable year to which this section applies40The fourth taxable year to which this section applies20.

children.label: i
children.text: the amount of assets set aside at the close of the first taxable year ending after , for purposes described in subsection (a), overJuly 18, 1984

children.label: ii
children.text: the account limit determined under this section (without regard to this paragraph) for the taxable year for which such increase is being computed.

children.heading: Funds to which paragraph applies
children.label: D Funds to which paragraph applies
children.text: This paragraph shall apply only to a welfare benefit fund which, as of , had assets set aside for purposes described in subsection (a).July 18, 1984

children.heading: Employer taxed on income of welfare benefit fund in certain cases
children.label: g Employer taxed on income of welfare benefit fund in certain cases

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: In the case of any welfare benefit fund which is not an organization described in paragraph (7), (9), or (17) of section 501(c), the employer shall include in gross income for any taxable year an amount equal to such fund’s deemed unrelated income for the fund’s taxable year ending within the employer’s taxable year.

children.heading: Deemed unrelated income
children.label: 2 Deemed unrelated income
children.text: For purposes of paragraph (1), the deemed unrelated income of any welfare benefit fund shall be the amount which would have been its unrelated business taxable income under section 512(a)(3) if such fund were an organization described in paragraph (7), (9), or (17) of section 501(c).

children.label: A
children.text: the amount of the tax imposed by this chapter which is attributable to the amount so included shall be treated as a contribution paid to such welfare benefit fund on the last day of such taxable year, and

children.label: B
children.text: the tax so attributable shall be treated as imposed on the fund for purposes of section 419(c)(4)(A).

children.heading: Mandatory aggregation
children.label: A Mandatory aggregation
children.text: For purposes of subsections (c)(4), (d)(2), and (e)(2), all welfare benefit funds of an employer shall be treated as 1 fund.

children.heading: Permissive aggregation for purposes not specified in subparagraph (A)
children.label: B Permissive aggregation for purposes not specified in subparagraph (A)
children.text: For purposes of this section (other than the provisions specified in subparagraph (A)), at the election of the employer, 2 or more welfare benefit funds of such employer may (to the extent not inconsistent with the purposes of this subpart and section 512) be treated as 1 fund.

children.heading: Treatment of related employers
children.label: 2 Treatment of related employers
children.text: Rules similar to the rules of subsections (b), (c), (m), and (n) of section 414 shall apply.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: i Regulations
children.text: The Secretary shall prescribe such regulations as may be appropriate to carry out the purposes of this subpart. Such regulations may provide that the plan administrator of any welfare benefit fund which is part of a plan to which more than 1 employer contributes shall submit such information to the employers contributing to the fund as may be necessary to enable the employers to comply with the provisions of this section.
